epigraph.text: The plans of the diligent lead surely to abundance.
epigraph.quote: The $t$-distribution is the small-sample counterpart to the normal.

content.text: We now construct confidence intervals for the population mean $\mu$.

content.title: $z$-Confidence Interval for $\mu$
content.content:
If $X_1, \ldots, X_n$ are from $\Normal(\mu, \sigma^2)$ with $\sigma$ known:
\[
 \xbar \pm z_{\alpha/2} \cdot \frac{\sigma}{\sqrt{n}}
\]
is a $(1-\alpha)$ confidence interval for $\mu$.
content.label: Theorem

content.text: In practice, $\sigma$ is rarely known. We estimate it with $S$.

content.title: $t$-Confidence Interval for $\mu$
content.content:
If $X_1, \ldots, X_n$ are from a normal population with $\sigma$ unknown:
\[
 \xbar \pm t_{\alpha/2, n-1} \cdot \frac{S}{\sqrt{n}}
\]
is a $(1-\alpha)$ confidence interval for $\mu$.
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Constructing a $t$-interval
content.problem:
A sample of 25 observations has $\xbar = 82$ and $S = 10$. Find a 
95\% confidence interval for $\mu$.

content.text: For large $n$ ($\geq 30$), the interval is robust to non-normality.
